description: Blind 8 per-event guest data export. Filtered to attended guests only. Direct PII removed. Name-based fields resolved to guest IDs.
fieldsTransformed: guests.referredBy → referredByGuestId (name resolved to guest ID, null if unresolved)
feedback.spendTimeWith → spendTimeWithGuestIds (comma-separated names resolved to guest ID array)

guests.passion: Sports - im less of a watcher and more of a player. I am excited join a fantasy league this year. Film and music are also easy to talk about endlessly.
guests.referralSource: +1 to Deepit at the coffee at opa event
guests.screeningNotes:
From DC, moved here last June, used to be a teacher and switched accounting, 

Why Join Blind 8:
- Social event (Opa), loves the structure, loves that Deepit is already there, loves the culture we've created, wants to meet new people in Austin, loves the vision for Blind 8

Last year, he went to Barcelona and stayed at a hostel for the first time, stuff going on all day, went out to a couple different clubs

guests.passion: Sports and concerts!

guests.whyJoin: Well, I ran into your TikTok and love this way you all are building community. Instead of watching it from my phone, i'd love to actually be apart of it and meet other cool people in my city.
guests.socialBravery: In my most recent years, speed dating. In my younger years, I did pageants which is not for the introverted or reserved. (Public speaking, interviews, etc.) but it pushed me out of my shell!
guests.passion: Definitely travel! Anyone who has a fun story or adventure to share, I could listen to for hours. I also love talking about atx and what people enjoy about living here. Animals, careers, hobbies. I'm open to it all. :)
guests.screeningNotes: Screening skipped — approved directly from application
guests.notes: Half black, half white, Been in Austin for 3 years,  works at wells fargo, her best friend is 41 years old, only did speed dating once, trying to get more into solo travels (a few times), birthday trip solo, never out of the country, connect her to Haison, planning to Edinborough, works an in-person job, made friends with coworkers and made friends through them, bigger city than Vegas sort of is, going workout classes,

guests.whyJoin: I just moved to Austin and I am looking to meet new people with similar interests and age! This looks like a great opportunity to do that, and to also meet people who are looking for some new friends.
guests.socialBravery: I studied abroad in Paris for 4 months and knew nobody. It was very scary because it’s a foreign country where not a lot of people speak English, and I also lived with a French family which was a very unique experienc.
guests.passion: I love to talk about movies, what’s coming out, etc. I love to talk about traveling especially in places such as Europe. Lastly I love to talk about marketing, brands, and what’s going on in the business world.
guests.screeningNotes: Moved to Austin in September, didn't really know anyone when he moved here, looking for different ways to meet new people, saw us walking around with our sign, seems like a cool opportunity to meet strangers, very normal to cling on to people you meet that are from US when you are studying abroad, small city called Annency,

guests.whyJoin: Just moved to Austin, looking for more friends and deeper connections. I’m very open minded and Blind8 dinners look AWESOME. I love the concept
guests.socialBravery: Marc Lore gave a presentation at my university and I convinced the admin to give me a spot at his private lunch and talked to him. He gave me advice that led me to secure my job opportunity.
guests.screeningNotes: saw our reels, looked into it for 5 minutes, moved here 2 and a half months ago, college experience during covid, so many young adults here, trains brazilian jiu jitsu, very chill guy, on the quieter/monotone side, moved from washington state, went to school in Minnesota, done 222
guests.notes: used to play chess competitively, working on his social skills, wants to be "elite" in everything he does, has never been to a hostel but would love to try it once

guests.whyJoin: I’d love to join a dinner with strangers because I genuinely enjoy meeting new people and hearing different perspectives. Some of the most interesting conversations happen when you sit at a table with people you’ve never met before. Everyone brings their own story, experiences, and ideas, and that creates a unique energy that you don’t get in your usual circle. I also believe that meaningful connections often start in simple moments like sharing a meal. Whether the conversation is about work, travel, hobbies, or life lessons, I’m always curious to learn from others and exchange ideas. It’s also a great way to step outside of routine, challenge your comfort zone, and build community in a natural way. I’d come with an open mind, positive energy, and genuine curiosity to connect, contribute to good conversation, and hopefully leave the table having learned something new from the people around me.
guests.socialBravery: I almost stayed home. Instead I showed up alone. Said hello to a stranger. That one brave moment turned into a table full of new friends.so it would be really rally cool
guests.passion: How travel changes people, third places, building communities, Disco and Funk House and Sprinkle of Bachata
guests.screeningNotes: Screening skipped — invited directly, from guatemala, very big fan of third spaces, he wants to be a Blind 8 host because he felt belonging, he's moved a lot to new cities, knows what it means to struggle to make friends - he wants to help people find their tribe

guests.passion: Baking, crafting, sustainability, and hanging out with friends !
guests.screeningNotes: From San Antonio, moved to austin a year ago, moved to city, works in recycling, was on Live Bait dating show, very good friends with Beyonce, helped her be more spontaneous, was shy and timid and feels like blind 8 brought her out of her shell

guests.whyJoin: Make friends in a new city and grow a community that I can depend on
guests.socialBravery: Honestly starting to post on tik tok and moving to a new city to start all over again
guests.screeningNotes:
Moved to Austin last May (almost here for a year), wanted a fresh start in life, fully remote job, from DC area, way too stale at home, decided to move to Austin, works in tech (entrepreneurial), went to school in Richmond, everyone he's met is friendly and easy to talk to, heard about through tiktok (doomscrolling one night - not going to sleep), solo traveled for 31st birthday, airbnb/hotel, traveled through south america, been to Portugal/Spain (favorite places in Europe), talking to them at restaurants & bars in Portugal, 

passionate about baking, tech, cooking (tiktok for cooking & baking), giving back to the community, politics, space tech(exploration), traveling, credit card usage

guests.whyJoin: Meet new people
guests.socialBravery: Traveling internationally by myself and making friends!

guests.whyJoin: I’m an extrovert and the crew sounds like fellow extroverts! I love spontaneous events and I am looking for find like minded people with diverse experiences and to build quality relationships with awesome people 😎
guests.socialBravery: I don’t have just one great example but my travels last year were Hawaii on 3 days notice, Chile on 5 day notice and Japan on 4. I’m spontaneous and always find a way to meet great people along the way.
guests.passion: Love to hear about people travels, diverse backgrounds, someone’s careers, or technology all the way to someone’s career.

I could talk about passion projects for hours - whether it's music, blogging, content creation, or even reading I feel like everyone has a way of expressing themselves that they're passionate about and I love hearing about it and talking about it!
guests.referralSource: I ran into you guys yesterday on town lake! Joking about Scott being a murderer
guests.screeningNotes: been in Austin for 2 years (April 2024), job after college (tech sales), feels "fine" going to Blind 8, has been to a lot of events where she doesn't know anyone else, mainly hang out with people from college (Baylor), has been wanting to make a community, needs a big friend group, has a lot of individual friends, pick up a lot of little passion projects, make youtube videos, quieter side, made jokes about Scott Garsson being a murderer when she met us lol

guests.passion: Sports and Movies

guests.whyJoin: I met Carlo and Alessandro at the Live Date Comedy Show and thought this was a really cool concept. Also my friend Joe told me to sign up because he thinks this will be a cool way for us to meet people.
guests.socialBravery: I think running for office as the age of 25. It was really scary and I had to be vulnerable past my usual limits and it made me a better person. Be trans and open about it can be a really scary thing in Texas
guests.passion: It’s a taboo but I love talking about politics and policy, since it’s the world I work in. I also love yapping about tv shows, I’m currently on a Korean drama kick
guests.screeningNotes: 2023, work in the legislature, decided to run for office, ran for state representative in Northwest side of Houston, endorsed by the Houston chronicle, starting her own consulting firm for young candidates, also hosts dinner parties (a lot more intimate lol), dinner parties for neighbors and friends, adopted into a mexican family, loves cooking soul food, got a dog back in Austin, meet a lot of people through her dog, met Joe (Live Bait ATX dating show) from his own gumbo party, she's in politics so we need to make sure that our hosts keep an eye on conversation during the dinner, believe in people having home-cooked meals, loves talking to people, all her friends are based around her job

guests.passion: Travel, cooking, baking, coffee, walks
guests.screeningNotes: Loves cooking, works at Oracle, we met her on the bridge and she was skeptical at first but appreciated my salesmanship lol, wants to build community in Austin, cooking is her love language, ran an operation in college cooking food for people

guests.whyJoin: Since I moved to Austin in Seeptember, I have rebuilt my life from scratch! So showing up alone to networking events became my specialty. I also saw you guys are Hispanic, and same!
guests.socialBravery: I show up to networking events that I am interested in alone. Whether it’s founders, creatives, RE, business anything. I can talk to anyone! A woman in the wild really. Oh and I am first gen eldest daughter. Should tell you everything!
guests.passion: I love talking life experiences and what makes you- you! Big on motivating others, and not feeling shame. Can talk about mindset for hours, and rewiring the brain/psychology. Alchemy energy<3
guests.screeningNotes: Moved from Dallas, whole life, Grays Disease Autoimmune Condition, ran a medspa in Austin, followed her intuition, Organic Clothing Line because of being allergic to Rayon (chemical processed fabric), the healthy life chose me, met Mexican-American co-founder the month she met here, very vulnerable, just doing women's clothes right now, trademarking, couple investors lined up, Called "Raiz", loungewear, sleepwear, underwear for women, loved to travel, love to dance, knows how to shake her hips, met people from Chile (took them out), used to travel always when she lived in Dallas to escape her life,

guests.passion: Somethings I like: Running / Walking around town lake, food, coffee, movies, stand up comedy, getting marginally better at guitar, well written books, draft beer, good people
guests.screeningNotes: interested in entrepreneurship as well

guests.whyJoin: I’m looking to meet new friends and date since I just moved to Austin. I don’t know anyone here or the area well so taking all the connections I can get :)
guests.socialBravery: I have solo traveled and backpacked in countries where I do not speak the language. I often just go to 3rd spaces and find folks that look interesting to strike up a conversation
guests.passion: I enjoy philosophy, music history, live performances, culture and media, social justice and volunteering
guests.screeningNotes: Tomorrow is 4 weeks, from the DC area, living in Memphis last two years for work, going to Spain the summer, picked her college because it had really good study abroad, graduated early and did backpacking, done some hostels (Prague for 6 weeks - lived in apartment), Budapest, homestay with a grandma in Argentina, likes doing apartments and homestays, couch surfer, went to Carnival in Uruguay with a stranger that she met in Argentina,

guests.whyJoin: Moved here this past summer and always looking to meet new people!
guests.socialBravery: Trusting a random man to give me my first tattoo in the lobby of my hostel on my first day in Portugal
guests.passion: EDM music! Love traveling for shows :) also my job/helping the underserved
guests.screeningNotes: Moved to Austin in July, from Buffalo, Parents are from Vietnam, first place she's lived besides home, in pharmacy residency, met a lot of people organically out at the bar, hard to maintain friendships with people, everyone is so busy, seeing our tiktoks, her and her best friend went to Portugal, booked trip 5 days before she left for the flight, only thing booked was hostel, stayed at a bunch of party hostels, the G spot in Lisbon, asked Brazilian guy if he was giving out massages, he was actually giving out tattoos, pair of dice tattoo, family's lucky number is 12, travels a lot within the United States, hasn't had a lot of time to travel abroad, hostels are cheaper, planning a trip to Spain in May, staying at hostels because she loves meeting new people, experience crazy things,

guests.whyJoin: I moved to Austin a few months ago and want to make new friends in the city and meet cool people! I also just want to learn about other people’s experiences and lives.
guests.socialBravery: I’ve tried Timeleft before for a few weeks and have gone to dinners consistently. I also have been at events alone and have talked to the people around me.
guests.passion: Music, different cultures, psychology and neuroscience, food, my friends, good books, hypothetical situations
guests.screeningNotes: Does marketing for Dell. From Chapel Hill. Born and raised there. Was a psych major/business major. Super interested in the human dynamic/brain side. Timeleft gripes: Was youngest at her table by a bit. Wants to be around younger people near her age. Was at many dinners that were just awkward. Was not making lasting connections. Went to a dinner every week.

guests.whyJoin: I’m in the “fuck yes” and “meet everyone” stage of my life! Really been enjoying expanding the friendships I have in this city. I’ve lived here for 6 years and have some close friends, but some are moving and some are starting to have kids so I’m figuring out what the next phase of connections looks like! It’s been really fun so far - and I love the vibe of the community you are creating
guests.socialBravery: Probably solo travel in general. I’ve shown up to countless hostels, bars, events, cities etc by myself and managed to make friends everywhere! The other side is just generally approaching other people - giving complements at the grocery store, handing out flowers on Valentine’s Day, etc - a small thing can feel so scary but really brighten someone’s day
guests.passion: Travel! I’ve been to every continent and ~37 countries (honestly I lose count), starting with international travel at age 10. I’ve done solo backpacking thru Europe and Southeast Asia, international trips with my 80 year old grandparents, and lots in between — I love sharing stories with others who have experienced the vast world out there!
guests.screeningNotes: Super sweet, found us on Instagram, moved here during Covid, intentionally nomadic for about a year, found somewhere to live more permanently, met in the bathroom

guests.occupation: Dancer, Slackliner, acro yogi, the arts
guests.whyJoin: I’m looking to expand my community and meet other cool people. I want to meet others that I could introduce to my community of dancers, slack liners, and acro yogies as well as be introduced to new communities. I love meeting people and it seems like I would be drawn to the types of people that are also drawn to this
guests.socialBravery: I went to an event full of strangers last week where you are put into small groups and then rotate. I meet some great people and I have already hung out with three of them separately outside of the event and two others are going to try out slacklining with me.
guests.passion: I love noticing how the relationships and friendships we’ve had with others have formed us. I love to go deep with people and understand how their experiences have shaped their viewpoints. On a lighter note… I love slacklining, dance, and acro yoga and enjoy introducing people to my hobbies. I also love to be a beginner and want to learn new things from others.
guests.screeningNotes: Alessandro

guests.whyJoin: All my life I've always been drive in taking risks and being spontaneous. From moving to Chile unexpectedly to doing random things in my free time, I've always liked to meet new people, cultures and stories that I could learn from. I'm always the friend in my group to create events, meetings and weird things to do to spicy up the friendship and make it not boring (:
guests.socialBravery:
Have two:
1. Dressing as Ed, Edd & Eddy for Halloween with my friends! Best memories and people loved it.
2. Gone to a thrift shop with my close friends in Houston (I'm from there too), choose each other outfits and then go eat to a fancy please or nice place hahaha loved it and really fun!
guests.screeningNotes: Been here for 3 years, spanish speaking, works at Dell. Friends with Justin from Austin #11
guests.notes: On a soccer team with like 30 people in Austin, he knew everyone at coquito, two little sisters, has a really cool tattoo on his right ankle of one big bear and two smaller bears behind (his sisters), half mexican, gentleman

guests.whyJoin: After moving to Austin in September , I’ve been focused on building a life here that feels aligned and full. I’m excited to meet women who value connection, curiosity, and creating memorable experiences together.
guests.socialBravery: Going to places by myself and putting myself in new environments, even when it feels uncomfortable. Choosing growth over staying in my comfort zone has been my biggest act of social bravery.
guests.passion: I can talk for hours about travel and how it reshapes your perspective. Also about what are the best shows to watch
guests.screeningNotes: been in Austin since september of last year, moved from El Paso (juarez), always wanted to leave her hometown, brother lives here in Austin (lives with her brother), I really don't know a lot of people in Austin and some of his friends, heard about Blind 8, new to the city, wants to meet new people, never done a solo trip, travel a lot with my family (visits her dad in California), this year I have a lot of trips planned, going to a lot of weddings in Mexico, trip to Europe change perspective (fast pace in life, everything in Europe is calmer), English is her second language, she's very sweet, a little a bit quieter, before she moved to Austin - she would always go with a friend, have gone to several gyms, would love to find a community, going to a friend's bachelor party in Mexico city (ask about that), wants to host because she wants to make people feel comfortable in their own discomfort,

guests.passion: Travel, food, Music, EDM/house music, DJ, golf, tennis, padel, entrepreneurship

guests.whyJoin: I’m a textbook extrovert who loves to chat with and learn about strangers - especially people in the arts. Still finding my people here in Austin, so new friends would be a great outcome. I’m also single, so always open for a new connection.
guests.socialBravery: I’ve moved every two to three years my whole life. I was a tour guide in college. I hosted and produced a standup comedy show in Austin for two years.
guests.passion:
1) Growing up and living abroad: Military brat who spent 6 years of childhood in Japan, did a Gap Year in Germany, and my undergrad in France. 

2) The Public Domain : What’s in there and why. 

3) Energy Production : History of energy and modern innovations
guests.screeningNotes: Friends with Cam Patterson, undergrad in Marseilles France, produced a stand up comedy show for 2 years, grad student at Florida (Go Gators), textbook extrovert, goes to networking events to relax, felt Blind 8 was intimate, wants to give the gift of time well spent to others

guests.whyJoin: I want to be a more outgoing person and make new lasting friends! I love exploring my city and would love to get to know people who want to do the same.
guests.socialBravery: I had shown up to parties alone and have made friends just from that! I also travel alone which requires much social bravery if I want to get to know others
guests.passion: The different books I’m reading and what I plan to head. I’m loving heated rivalry right now too and go on for hours analyzing the show and books
guests.screeningNotes: Moved to Austin 2 years ago for work, came across our video on tiktok/instagram, moved from DFW, born in Brazil, northern part of Brazil, Recife, planning to go to Brazil for new years this upcoming year, loves to go out on the weekend with her friends (Barbs & East 6th), follow wherewhenwhat, travel every week for work by herself, make time to explore the cities by herself, just went to LA (drove to Manhattan beach), local coffee shops & restaurants, Italy trip planned in June with her friend, event coordinator for medical education company, her apartment threw a DJ set and she went up to them and talked to them, sleetball fight with strangers that she met at the DJ set, on the more meek side

guests.passion:
The resurgence of RuneScape (video game), why it’s so successful, and nostalgia

Economic challenges for young people, gambling, and nihilism

The difficulty of getting a weekend golf tee time in Austin
guests.referralSource: Austin Perkins (my roommate) brought me as a plus 1 to Parlor and Yard Happy Hour. Had a great time after he left and hung on with yall for hours!
guests.screeningNotes: Super sweet, mellow dude. Came to Blind 8 happy hour - was a little scared but pushed himself to keep going and ended up sticking around even after. Went to rave runners with Diego. Plays softball in a rec league. In a pickleball league on Tuesdays. Has been in Austin 2 years. Originally form Tampa, went to UF, lived in Cedar Rapids Iowa for a few years. Super good conversationalist - very easy to talk to.

guests.whyJoin: I love meeting people, hearing their stories, and building real connections beyond small talk. I enjoy connecting with strangers and discovering how our experiences overlap and universalize.
guests.socialBravery: Honestly my line of work as a therapist. Helping break stigma, holding space for people to share painful things, and creating a safe place for real honesty.
guests.passion: Deep conversations about life, psychology, therapy, and the meaning behind our experiencesDeep conversations about life, psychology, therapy, and the meaning behind our experiences.
guests.screeningNotes: she's note

guests.occupation: Entrepreneur in Real Estate, AI education and youth development.
guests.whyJoin: Love the idea of bringing strangers together. Huge see follower of Yes theory and Discover Connection, and this seems to follow suite. Plus, candidly, I am hoping to find someone not on a dating app 🤪
guests.socialBravery:
Backpacking with a guitar to 25+ countries and bringing people together at hostels for live karaoke sessions + hitting open mics whenever they come up. 

Favorite memory by far was hiking to Maccho Pichu in Peru and jamming out in a random tent in the middle of nowhere with a group of strangers also trying to survive the 50 mile trek
guests.screeningNotes: Used to own a bike shop. Seems like a super nice guy. graduated from school when he was 19. Big big traveler. Born and raised in Austin. Manages 4 houses - big into real estate. Super spontaneous. Got into Latin dance - now a latin dance instructor and in the music scene. 7
guests.notes: adventurer, loves to go to a city and travel 1 hr outside of the city and talk to locals, wants to hitchhike across the atlantic, hitchhiked from Croatia to Venice, built a 2 story house in Alaska, salsa instructor, drives a tesla, graduated college at 19yrs old

guests.whyJoin: I recently moved back to Austin so I want to make friends and build my community. I love hosting things, being spontaneous, meeting new people, so I'm excited to try something new!
guests.socialBravery: I don't have a specific example off the top of my head, but I tend to just say hi to new people at parties and try to make new friends wherever I am. I went out to a bar once and ended up joining a group to go to a rave afterward.
guests.passion: Anything art, my research, board games!! I love learning about people so I just ask a bunch of random questions haha (no specific topics)
guests.screeningNotes: masters in Houston, from Austin (moved here in 8th grade), born in Cary, North Carolina, very pretty, masters in BME (cancer biology), interviewing for med school right now, currently lives with family here in Austin, loves hosting board game nights & mariokart nights, very much the snack friend, loves hosting chill stuff, artsy stuff with girl friends, 20 different board games (favorite is pandemic & codenames/secret hitler), very energetic, out with one other friend at a club, one of my friends was being hit on so they went to the rave with them, when you go to raves (they'll have fake plant clips), very bubbly, will feel intimidated coming in so will need a helping hand right at the beginning of the dinner, how do you feel going to a dine

guests.whyJoin: I love to hear people’s stories: where they come from, what makes them smile, why they get out of bed in the morning. As someone who has moved around a lot, I have had people come and go in my life. Yet, bits and pieces of the people I meet sta with me through the stories they shared with me. I figured blind 8 might expose me to people I would not meet or befriend otherwise in my UT bubble.
guests.socialBravery:
Being vulnerable!! 
Also going up to people in public and striking up a conversation
guests.screeningNotes: Abroad in Paris, semester abroad, June - end of December, French, finding out about teach for America, wants to do publishing for penguin random house, used to be pre law, ask people why they’re there,
guests.notes: Doing a thesis on Political Influence on Water Crisis in Mexico City, Plan 2 at UT Austin, studied abroad in Paris, younger sister (16 years old)

guests.whyJoin: Id love to join since I moved to ATX not too long ago but have found it quite hard to make friends outside of work and gym. This seemed like the best opportunity to do so, plus my friend mentioned how great the experience was!
guests.socialBravery:
My best example of social bravery was definitely getting pitched on my friends dating show back home in
Seattle, WA (fun&flirty). Keep in mind she pitched me multiple times and I said yes out of pure pressure and for good exposure/show content for social media.
guests.passion: Anything art related! Video games, new music, photography, new movies/tv shows, concerts and also new tech!
guests.screeningNotes: Hard to make a friend group, 9 months ago, has a girlfriend, outside of work & gym, downloaded Timeleft (never went through it), was on a comedy dating show (powerpoint presentation), just got a job working in tech sales, zendesk

guests.passion: Anime, Movies, TV shows, Japan, Mexico, CDMX, Music Festivals, Rock, Talking  Shit, Audiobooks, Reading, Air fryer, Cumbia dancing, Kickball, Arm wrestling, Hostels, Defending the weak, Stopping injustices, Weightlifting, Orange Theory Fitness, Lifetime, Sauna, Taking many pictures, Podcasts, Travel, Europe, Investing, Making tons of pesos, Tacos
guests.screeningNotes: From monterrey, studied International business, got MBA at Michigan, moved to texas for better Tacos, works from home a lot and was feeling alone, needed community,

guests.whyJoin: I moved to Austin in July and I’m looking for a community. I feel like I lost that when I moved here. I’d like to get to know people that are active and like to explore
guests.socialBravery: I would like to think this is a form of social bravery. I once took myself on a month-long trip to Spain after a breakup. I felt lost and wanted to find myself again. By doing so I found my own bravery. Now I love doing things solo on occasion.
guests.passion: I really enjoy getting to know people on a deeper level. I also find history fascinating. I love coffee and the outdoors.
Travel, F1, entrepreneurship, Lemonade, Sports
guests.screeningNotes: Runs a podcast called Side Street, has interviewed and volunteers for Coffee & Chill here in Austin (wellness social community), has not been to a hostel yet but wants to quit job in ideal world and go on a solo trip, has a crush on the girl in the cowboy hat who went to the ranch party

guests.whyJoin: I moved to Austin eight months ago and I'm tired of surface-level happy hours. I want to sit across from someone and actually learn what keeps them up at night. The idea of a curated dinner with strangers who are equally open to that sounds like exactly what I've been looking for.
guests.socialBravery: Last month I went to an improv class alone knowing absolutely no one. I was terrified the entire drive there but ended up doing a scene about being a lost penguin. I've gone back three times since. Before that I traveled solo to Mexico City and made friends at a mezcal bar by just sitting at the communal table and asking people what they were drinking.
guests.passion: I've been really into urban sketching lately — sitting in coffee shops and drawing strangers. There's something meditative about observing people without them knowing.
guests.screeningNotes: Alessandro is super nice, very nervous, let's make sure to sit him near a host so that he feels included in the conversation at all times.

guests.whyJoin: I love meeting people, learning about someone new, I’ve been wanting to meet new people in Austin and this seems like a perfect option, and I also love talking
guests.socialBravery: Moving to a different country and making friends from 0. Making friends it’s already hard sometimes, so adding being in a new place, different language, everything different it’s just another level
guests.passion: I could talk about music for hours, music has always been part of my life, also my friends that are like family to me, so many stories and memories.
guests.screeningNotes: Really great she lives blind 8 and believes in it

guests.whyJoin: I’ve lived in Austin for about a year and half and am ready to grow and meet new people outside of my colleagues. I am a very social person and find the idea of Blind8 super exciting!
guests.socialBravery: In December 2024 I decided to move to Austin from Seattle. I had only been once before for a concert. I came to the city in January 2025 with my two cats, and didn’t know a single person! Since then I’ve been challenging myself to do things out of my comfort zone and really dedicate myself to hobbies (like volleyball, and two step) to make new friends and find community.
guests.passion: I love talking about current events, history, music, and sports! I am a huge fan of football and soccer, country music, and having heart to hearts about life and our unique stories and experiences.
guests.screeningNotes: Born in Russia, lived in Ukraine for a bit, knows Justin from Austin #11, volunteers with Austin Pets Alive, getting into basketball, wants to do paddleboarding & march madness events, lived in south korea

guests.whyJoin: I absolutely LOVE this concept. As mentioned in the last question, meeting new people is truly one of my favorite things. I love a good heart to heart convo, hearing different peoples perspectives and experiences, and having a genuine time.
guests.socialBravery: I would say selling all of my things and moving to London by myself. I didn’t even know my flat mate; the first time I officially met her was the day I arrived at her door with my bags!
guests.passion: Traveling experiences! I’m a huge solo traveler and my biggest passion is traveling, meeting new people and exploring new cultures/countries. I recently just moved back to Austin after living in London solo for 7 months.
guests.screeningNotes: 2 months solo travelling around europe after college, went to Texas State University, visited London a couple times for my birthday :), associate event producer for an experiential marketing agency, live events & brand events, going to Sips & Sounds, was in London for 7 months, has an "okay" british accent, found herself getting into a little bit of a lull, Blind 8 helped her overcome her travel addiction, Blind 8 gives her cool hostel vibes

guests.whyJoin: I’m originally from North Dakota  and went to school in the Midwest. I was involved with many clubs and loved making connections and friends wherever I go! After I moved to Austin, I’ve been looking for new ways to make meaningful connections and new friends. I love spontaneous adventures and I love the idea behind Blind8! I’m glad I found yall!
guests.socialBravery: I challenge myself to take one solo trip a year in order to grow and learn more about myself and the culture I immerse myself in. I  love staying in hostels, making new friends, and going on new adventures.

guests.passion: I love working out, currently into CrossFit . Also traveling to tropical places and just enjoy life, meet new people and be with friends

guests.whyJoin:
I moved to Austin on February 1st and decided pretty quickly I wasn’t interested in doing the whole “ease into it” thing. I’d rather throw myself into the deep end and see who I meet there.

What I actually enjoy is the moment when a conversation flips. When someone drops the polished version of themselves and says something real, and suddenly the whole table leans in.

Most social settings don’t get you there. They hover in the safe zone.

I’m drawn to spaces where people are willing to skip a few steps, where curiosity is mutual, and where strangers are open to surprising themselves a little. That’s the kind of energy I look for and the kind I naturally bring.
guests.socialBravery:
A pattern I’ve noticed in myself is that I don’t really wait for permission to engage.

If I’m in a room where I don’t know anyone, I’ll pick someone, walk over, and start a conversation that’s actually worth having. Not “what do you do,” but something that makes them pause for a second.

I’ve had moments where that turns into an hour-long conversation with someone I would’ve otherwise never met, just because I chose not to stay in the background.

"}

applications.answers: {"whyJoin":"I’d love to join a dinner with strangers because I genuinely enjoy meeting new people and hearing different perspectives. Some of the most interesting conversations happen when you sit at a table with people you’ve never met before. Everyone brings their own story, experiences, and ideas, and that creates a unique energy that you don’t get in your usual circle. I also believe that meaningful connections often start in simple moments like sharing a meal. Whether the conversation is about work, travel, hobbies, or life lessons, I’m always curious to learn from others and exchange ideas. It’s also a great way to step outside of routine, challenge your comfort zone, and build community in a natural way. I’d come with an open mind, positive energy, and genuine curiosity to connect, contribute to good conversation, and hopefully leave the table having learned something new from the people around me.","guestType":"listener","socialBravery":"I almost stayed home. Instead I showed up alone. Said hello to a stranger. That one brave moment turned into a table full of new friends.so it would be really rally cool","passion":"I like to tell stories, I like to farm, vibe meet cool people. I also play soccer and watch live sports"}
applications.qualificationNotes: Strong connection-focused motivation. Compelling social bravery example
